preview_text:
The model favors Uruguay at 74%, with Cape Verde at a modest 5% to upset on a neutral ground. The gap reflects a substantial differential in the underlying strength estimates, leaving the underdog with limited paths to victory.

The outcome distribution tilts decisively toward Uruguay, yet the draw carries meaningful likelihood at 21%. A narrow home-field effect is absent at a neutral venue, so the probabilities hinge on relative team quality alone. The match sits between lopsided and competitive—Uruguay enters as clear favorite but not overwhelming.

The model and market diverge meaningfully on Cape Verde's chances. Sportsbooks assign 12%, roughly double the model's 5%. The two sources align closely on Uruguay's win probability (model 74%, market 65%, Polymarket 66%) and are nearly identical on draws (21–23%). The dashboard does not adjudicate which reading of Cape Verde's strength is more accurate; the divergence warrants attention from users assessing tail risk.
